headline: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike Stocks Pop After Anthropic Announces Partnerships
summary: ‘AI models have reached a level of coding capability where they can surpass all but the most skilled humans at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities,’ Anthropic says.

headline: Anthropic touts AI cybersecurity project with Big Tech partners
summary: Anthropic on Tuesday announced an initiative with major technology companies, including Amazon.com, Microsoft and Apple, that lets partners preview ‌an advanced model with cybersecurity capabilities developed by the AI startup.  Under ‌its "Project Glasswing", select organizations will be allowed to use the startup's unreleased and general-purpose AI model, "Claude ​Mythos Preview", for defensive cybersecurity work, Anthropic said.  Other partners include CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Google and Nvidia.

headline: Cylake Appoints Board of Directors and Secures Additional Strategic Investment
summary: Board brings decades of experience guiding cybersecurity at global scale, while In-Q-Tel (IQT) makes a strategic investmentSUNNYVALE, Calif., April 07, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cylake today announced the formation of its Board of Directors, alongside an additional strategic investment supporting the company’s development. headline: Is It Time To Reassess Palo Alto Networks (PANW) After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether Palo Alto Networks at around US$161.95 is priced for opportunity or already reflects its strengths, this article will walk through what the current share price might be saying about value. The stock has moved 4.9% over the last 7 days, with returns of a 1.9% decline over 30 days, a 9.7% decline year to date, and gains of 6.2% over 1 year, 68.2% over 3 years, and 173.4% over 5 years. Recent headlines around Palo Alto Networks have focused on its role in...

headline: Zscaler's AI Agent Security Push: Will It Be a New Growth Driver?
